I have played this game for over 1700 hours, and the only reason I am still playing is because I bought a premium vehicle. Without premium, the progression is painfully slow, frustrating, and simply not fun. To be honest, I would have never kept playing War Thunder if I hadn’t spent money on it. The game is extremely unbalanced, especially when it comes to battle ratings and vehicle matchmaking. Fighting late Cold War tanks or jets with World War II vehicles is not just unrealistic, it’s straight-up stupid. This has been a problem for years, and the developers clearly don’t care. Instead of fixing core issues like balance and progression, the developers focus on selling overpriced vehicle packs that now cost 80€ or more. For that price, you can buy a full, high-quality game like Red Dead Redemption 2 — and that says everything about how greedy this monetization has become. It honestly feels like the developers are more interested in milking the player base than improving the game. On top of that, there are even rumors about AI-generated profile pictures and vehicle camouflages, which just shows how little effort is being put into actual content. In its current state, I cannot recommend this game to new players. Unless you are willing to spend money early on, the experience is frustrating, unbalanced, and designed to push you toward opening your wallet.

I have played this game for years (before getting it on steam recently, I've easily got 4,000 hours on it), it has always had a level of potential that could be considered unrecognized greatness, but it always has fallen short of the goal. It's always 'almost fun'. Top tier can be described as COD point and click adventure where armor barely matters, and balance is a myth. It's also inundated with Russian bias, but likely not in the way you might imagine. It isn't 'Oh Russian stuff is just overpowered', although occasionally they will release some very broken Russian equipment (Like the 2S38 or the BMPTs). The Russian bias comes from how they handle documents and reports. They **refuse** to believe things that Russia can't do, for example their official stance on the Stinger/Mistral/Type91 missiles performing poorly is quite literally 'Igla can't do it therefore they can't.' So that is the level of Russian bias you should expect. They firmly believe the Type 10 MBT the Japanese produced cannot have armor because Russia can't make something that light also be armored, they believe the trunnion is hollow because once again Russia can't do it. They haven't even modeled the CVT on said tanks because once again, unknown technology. This kind of slavic lens paints every single action they make on the game, it makes western equipment subpar for annoying reasons. Not to mention anything Russian performs **perfectly** right out of the box almost every single time, and any issues are corrected immediately. Meanwhile every other faction that isn't Germany or America (The big three) tends to suffer for extended periods of time. Overall the game is ruined by laziness, greed, and their own personal bias painting their supposedly realistic game. Where Relikt can magically stop a Maverick from above. I, personally, just want better game modes. I wanted a top tier mode like enduring conflict with respawns that is fun, or a squad v squad air realistic mode instead of the missile madness mode we have at top tier currently. Instead we never get new modes. You know what we do get? Copy pasted premiums for $70. Some anime garbage with AI generated slop textures for more than the price of a full game. I cannot condone these practices, and it is time Gaijin suffers for this, I do not recommend anyone play War Thunder until the company grows a better work ethic.

Note that this review is primarily from the perspective of a USSR/Germany/Britain Ground RB player with some Air RB experience in the same trees. Undisclosed AI generated textures and cosmetics, a new wave of copy-pasted tanks and jets at top tier, overpowered premiums somehow more obvious than ever, with top-tier being virtually unplayable thanks to BMPTs and similar Russian premium vehicles and CAS aircraft. The gameplay is extremely inconsistent, with volumetric shells and exceedingly buggy armour and damage models leading to frustrating gameplay. Some tech-trees are affected more consistently than others, with the British and other minor nations generally getting the short end of the stick compared to the USA, Germany and USSR at virtually every tier of gameplay. The map design is some of the worst I've ever seen in a video game, if not the worst. Team Fortress 2 community maps and classic Doom deathmatch maps are more balanced and considered than the utter CQB slop War Thunder has to offer. The maps are usually tiny, only a few KM in any direction, and larger maps (not by much) only really become available at higher BRs and in Simulator mode. Spawns will often have nearly direct line of sight on one another and many are under 1km apart. Many sections of the map are blocked off in spite of being in bounds, as they used to be overpowered positions at a previous time that players took advantage of. Similarly, tank traction is significantly nerfed to prevent "Mountain-goating", where players would reach overpowered or undefeatable positions by driving up steep inclines, instead of JUST blocking these areas off or redesigning maps with massive issues. Having played the game for nearly 1,000 hours on Xbox before getting a PC, I used to think there was no such thing as Russian Bias, and that most of the problems in the game were equally felt by all players and all tech-trees, but I no longer believe this to be the case. Gaijin have always been content to introduce overpowered vehicles, especially as Premiums and Pack vehicles (which can cost anywhere up to €70 depending on their BR), and it so happens that most of these overpowered vehicles, whether or not they're premium, are on the USSR/Russian tech tree. This is most obvious now with the introduction and refusal to adequatly nerf the BMPT "Terminators" and greatly overrepresented Russian missiles, such as LMURs. Additionally, many aspects of historical vehicles are misrepresented for "Balance" purposes, mostly things like reload and turret slewing rates, which are seemingly completely subject to Gaijin's interpretation and discretion. Realism in all walks of the game is subjective. You can likely find videos explaining the many ways that War Thunder is selectively realistic and how it disproportionately benefits Russian vehicles. There are some positives to War Thunder, however. The graphics are pretty good, and the game runs well at medium settings even on my unimpressive PC. There is also practically no alternative to the game, as World of Tanks offers a completely different experience. If there was an alternative to War Thunder, it would put pressure on the Devs to bring their product up to scratch. The core concept of a vehicle combat game with realistically modelled armour, ammunition and components instead of a glorified health-bar like WoTs' is interesting and fun. Ultimately, I enjoy and have enjoyed playing War Thunder over the last 4 or so years, but I can't recommend that anybody so much as tries the game. It is not worth your time. I have played every tier of Ground RB gameplay for at least a small amount of time, and done so to varying degrees across multiple tech-trees, and I wish I had spent that time doing something else.

I'm not one to normally leave reviews, but honestly I like Warthunder, it's a game I enjoyed, but I'm not going to put the effort into reviewing a game if the developers won't even put effort into the game, so here's a page out of Gajin's book, the rest of this review is created with Gemini, like most of the game it seems. I’ve been playing *War Thunder* for years, and watching this game slowly decay into a soulless cash grab has been incredibly depressing. What used to be a detailed, passion-driven military simulator has officially crossed the threshold into corporate laziness, and the developers at Gaijin seem more interested in cutting corners than fixing their fractured game. The absolute breaking point has been the encroaching use of AI. It’s insulting as a customer to pay $70+ for premium vehicles only to find out that the company can’t even be bothered to pay actual human artists to texture and design them. Discovering AI placeholders, weird artifacting, and literal AI watermarks (like the Gemini logos left on in-game assets and profile icons) shows exactly where their priorities lie. When called out, their response isn't to hire talented artists to fix it—it’s to just scrub the watermarks and leave the cheap AI slop in the game. When a company stops respecting its own art department, you know they’ve completely stopped respecting the players. Aside from the AI controversies, the actual gameplay loop is getting worse with every consecutive update. * **The Broken Meta Pipeline:** The monetization model has become outright predatory. Gaijin routinely drops a blatantly overpowered, broken premium vehicle (like the recent BMP-T or various top-tier jets), watches the community buy it en masse to survive the meta, and then quietly nerfs it into the ground six months later after they've milked every dollar out of it. * **Constant UI and Economy Regression:** The interface updates over the last year have been atrocious, overcomplicating things that worked perfectly fine. Meanwhile, core game modes have been stale for half a decade. We are still playing the same repetitive team deathmatches on terrible, unbalanced maps where you can get sniped directly into your own spawn from across the map two minutes into a match. * **The Soul-Crushing Grind:** Unless you are running premium time and high-tier premium vehicles, progressing through a single tech tree takes a ridiculous, agonizing amount of time. Instead of making the game rewarding to play, Gaijin treats its player base like data points to be optimized for maximum frustration-spending. Don't feed the Snail. *War Thunder* has completely lost its way, trading genuine craftsmanship and historical passion for automated asset generation and endless premium slop. Save your sanity, your time, and your wallet, and play something else.

I have a lot more hours than my Steam account shows. I usually play through the launcher but I have just enough playtime on here to leave a review. I'm near level 100 and while I don't have an exact hour count on hand, it's 'a lot'. I mostly play tanks. 10.0 BR with UK, 9.7 with Germany, 9.0 with U.S., 8.3 with USSR. Nothing toptier but, well, for a lot of reasons I'm not in a rush to get there. But I can't recommend this game right now. Ongoing issues that have not been properly fixed like the map design being 90% spawntrap hell, with really cheeky spots where you can just snip players across maps with little to no counterplay. Volumetric absorbing entire shells to the point they do none of the damage they should. New vehicles that upset the balance of things, especially in toptier like the BMPTs that you can purchase for real money, and a host of bugs and audio issues that seem to change with each and every hotfix. And, of course, the grind. But instead of fixing any of this, they instead just release copy-pasted vehicles in the guise of new and exciting updates, increasing the amount of premium vehicles compared to the free 'tech tree' vehicles, pumping out goonbait skins (that aren't even really goonbait: there's nothing risque on the skins themselves so unless you have a VERY vivid imagination while playing your war vehicle game it ain't doing it for you chief) for the price of a full AAA game, and can't even be bothered to make those skins original: They use AI to generate the skins. In fact they use AI for a lot of things in War Thunder, turns out. To the point I'm not even sure what *isn't* AI in the game now. Now I'm not one of those who decries AI entirely: I think in things like the medical field AI is capable of a lot of good. It's when it's used to replace the human creative process where I have a big issue. Even with how bad things currently are in the game, especially in top tier where Terminators just spawncamp you in a manner of minutes, I was still able to squeeze fun out of it because I played vehicles I enjoyed. Now I can't really support it purely on a principle level, and I regret what I did support in regards to premium time and vehicles. I don't know where that money is going, but it's not going to War Thunder. And if they aren't in any rush to improve the game and instead milk players for all they're worth because a woman's voice gets them all excited, well, I'm not really interested. War Thunder needs competition. It's sadly the only game of its type, and nothing really replaces it. I guess I'll wait patiently for that day, because I don't think I'm spending any more time with this AI slopfest.

War Thunder, ten spyte van sy ernstige foute en gebrek aan tienks en opdaterings wat veranderinge aan balans maak en die spel meer realisties maak om die gemeenskap tevrede te stel, sê ek wat ek daarvan hou. War Thunder, selfs met ernstige gebreke, het die potensiaal om goed te wees. Ek kan sonder probleme speel, selfs al speel ek nie gereeld nie en speel ek net 'n paar keer; ek geniet dit en ly, wat normaal is vir hierdie spel. Wat ek regtig wil hê, is tenks van Suid-Afrika en Duitsland, en die bestaan ​​​​​van Roemeense en Hongaarse tenks en die van die Balkan. Selfs al is hulle vreemd en min bekend, moet hulle in die navorsingsboom wees, benewens die wys van 'n min bekende tenk en dit beroemd te maak. Dit verhoog die aantal tenks van sommige lande, soos Duitsland en die Verenigde Koninkryk, soos ek voorheen genoem het. En wat ek ook saamstem, moet wees dat daar 'n IS-2 of T-34-85 is wat deur die Duitsers gevange geneem is. Selfs al is dit baie ongebalanseerd, daar is goeie tenks soos die Tiger H1 en E is, en die Panther-reeks, moet Duitsland tenks ontvang wat van die Geallieerdes gevange geneem is. Dit voeg 'n historiese toon aan die spel, wat nog altyd die bedoeling daarvan was.

DO NOT download this game. I don't care how cool the TikTok clips look or how much your friends beg you to squad up. Just stay away... I’m writing this from the bottom of the rabbit hole. I have thrown 2,100 hours of my life and wayyy more than $400 into this black hole, after only trying it playing f2p. I ground out top-tier tanks and jets for the US, Germany, and Russia. My first was Germany, without any premium account nor vehicles to the top, completely f2p. The others with lineups of premium vehicles... What do I have to show for it? Zero actual joy, a mountain of regret, and a textbook case of gaming Stockholm syndrome. War Thunder is just a frustration machine built to make you spend real money to fix problems the developers intentionally created! Forget "Russian Bias". The reality is way worse. Gaijin doesn't care about flags and petty national pride; they are just a corporate machine using engineered imbalance to print cash. The broken volumetric armor, the magical fuel tanks that swallow shells, and the ghost rounds are coded on purpose to make you lose your mind. They want you to feel completely powerless so you look at the thousands of hours left on the grind, crack, and pull out your credit card. But the second you buy a $70 premium to escape the misery, the matchmaker throws you straight into a premium sweat-shop bracket, your vehicle gets secretly nerfed in a minor patch, and you are right back to being food. They actively tank the economy and slash rewards so you barely make enough in-game cash to cover basic repair costs unless you run a premium account and a premium vehicle at the exact same time. ESPECIALLY if you have skill issues. Not me tho, I'm built different... jk- Top-tier ground is pure anti-fun. You spend months unlocking a modern tank, only for it to spawn in completely broken with no laser rangefinder, no thermals, and no fire extinguishers. You will literally sit there and burn to death from a minor engine scratch because you didn't pay the digital tax to unlock a basic fire extinguisher. Then you deal with level-one players who buy high-rank premium packs, rush and die once because they don't know the maps, and quit immediately, leaving your team half-empty in three minutes. If you survive that, Close Air Support vets or whales bomb you from kilometers away while you sit in a tank with absolutely no way to shoot back, all on tiny arcade maps made a decade ago for WWII Shermans. The current developer cycle is a joke. They drop copy-pasted tech tree vehicles AND/OR a broken, overpowered premium &/ top-tier vehicle, let it ruin the matchmaking for months so everyone buys it out of desperation, and the moment sales slow down, they nerf it under the guise of "historical balancing" before dropping the next broken vehicle to start the loop over. Reminder that they once firmly stated they'll never let players buy to the top... The laziness has reached an absolute breaking point. Look at the recent Senrai Maiden packs. They are charging $70 for copy-paste vehicle clones that already exist for free in the normal tech tree, wrapping them in cheap anime gooner skins. They don't even pay real human artists anymore. The community caught them red-handed leaving visible Google Gemini AI watermarks on the premium decorations and M60 camouflage textures. When caught, they blamed an "outsourcing partner", erased the watermark, and left the exact same AI slop in the paid packs anyway. From profile pictures to voice lines, they use generative AI to cut production costs to zero while charging you luxury collector prices. They've done this for a while now, with all the AI generated profile pictures you can buy, that they've slapped an "artist" name on... Even the company structure is a mess. Huge whistleblower leaks from long-time staff like Keofox exposed that fired employees kept admin access to internal player data and Steam backends for years. They even admitted that Gaijin explicitly codes pop-up review prompts to only trigger right after a player wins a match or gets a lucky prize, intentionally rigging the metrics to mask how miserable the player base actually is on Steam. The ultimate tragedy is that War Thunder is a monopoly holding military history nerds like us hostage. It sits in the perfect dead zone between the arcade nonsense of World of Tanks and the stressful complexity of DCS. Because there is zero competition, Gaijin knows you have nowhere else to go. They actively exploit your love for aviation and armor, trapping it behind a digital casino layout. They ignore every plea for new game modes, bigger battlefields, or a tank-only mode because they know there is always a new wave of players willing to take the abuse. They do not care about history, balance, or whether you are actually having fun. They care about keeping you trapped on a treadmill of digital misery just long enough to bleed your wallet dry- -~-~-~-~-~-~-~- If your love for military history is too strong and you ignore this warning anyway, do yourself one favor: treat this game strictly like a digital museum. Play the low tiers, appreciate the WWII armor, and uninstall the absolute second you hit Rank III. ~The moment you chase the modern era, the historical simulator ends, and the predatory progression wall takes over. Break the cycle before it starts. Save your sanity, and play literally anything else~

After over 600 hours of this game I have both good and bad things to say about this game, with the latter unfortunately becoming an overwhelming majority. Let's start with the good things, as this part will be short. - The game itself is quite fun, especially in the lower tiers - I have reached at most 6.7 br, but I honestly don't see it getting much more fun as I am an enthusiast of ww2 armor. - The combat feels mostly good (more on this in the bad part). You can feel the weight of most of the tanks, the ballistics are easy to grasp and the kills are satisfying. - Graphics are spectacular - From my experience with plane combat in this game - the flight models are quite well done. This game started out as a air combat game, so this is understandable. - Naval is quite fun, unfortunately not very popular, there's a whole meme about there only being 3 naval players in the world Now for the bad part: - The grind is absolutely atrocious. If you do not use a whole truckfull of boosters or you don't buy the premium account after around 5.7 br, you might go a week or more without a new vehicle (depending on how much you play) - The volumetric shell system, which was supposed to help with the fact that before it was introduced, you could pixel hunt weaknesses in armor, brought it's own problems. Now the shells have their volume. If you hit an intersection of armor plates, even if your shell should absolutely melt through them, the game might just calculate a non-pen because it seems like it just sums up the armor values of the two plates (it's probably more sophisticated but that is what it feels like at times). You would think that this is a chance one in a thousand. It is not, you will have several of hits like this per match most likely, even if you aim precisely for your shots. - Shell types being broken after years - HESH, this topic you should probably just watch a gameplay video of the FV4005 and see for yourself the kind of bullsh that happens. - And on the top of this - the devs, instead of fixing any of those problems, prefer to release 70 dollar packs of copy paste vehicles with Gemini generated textures WHICH THEY DIDNT EVEN REMOVE THE WATERMARK FROM and anime girls riding them which ALSO have AI generated voices. (They also use AI for profile pic icons and much much more - ive even seen it used for a CAMO PATTERN) Even if we disregard those recent releases, it has been a problem for a very long time that premium vehicles are simply better than the tech tree ones. On the previous topic of HESH, the american premium T58 does not seem to suffer from the same issues that the FV and many other do. - The whole thing about how at some point fast vehicles were able to get into scummy camp spots so the devs instead of, oh I don't know, fixing those spots???? decided to change the traction characteristics on every tank so now every larger hill feels like it's covered in soap TLDR: Play this game if you like the devs not caring about their game being broken and instead releasing overpriced AI slop STEAM WHY IS THERE NO AI USAGE TAG ON THE PAGE OF THIS GAME

The most realistic "the other way" tank game, where you take down jet fighters with a howitzer, rush with a gunned truck against heavy tanks or fight as israel alongside nazis on "realistic battles". Yeah very realistic... But well, that's honestly the smallest problem of the game and it actually can be fun to use vehicles as not intended or to see such absurdities. The problem is with the developer and how they mistreat their very flagship and, as of the recent, beating their own records in being a spineless greedy bas- I mean eh, custard... Vell anyway. It's playable, possibly still the best warfare game that joins tanks and airplanes and naval together to this date, but it is in disrepair and grossly neglected. Really do not waste money on it... Or honestly dunno if even just playing the game is okay, I don't feel like touching it anymore even though I'm a total free-to-play. Okie this ramble aside, here ze reasons: - Pay-to-Win, still a lot (as there was an improvement few years ago) of grinding has to be done in order to get to the top tiers (After basically a year of casual / weekend gaming (Returned to WarThunder May 2025) I have just reached 8.0 BR, if I didn't touch other tech trees as well I could be perhaps at 8.7 (wow so large diff)). The notorious bushes are usually bought with Golden Eagles aka premium currency (they're rarely obtainable by other means) and give the bearer huge advantage especially when combined with smaller tanks & low BR "realistic" battles - High tiers are in turn full of cheaters and extremely toxic people sending death threats (TF2 community moment anyone?) - Damage models are still broken after years, like tracks absorbing entire damage, bullets that randomly decide to deal no damage in spite of perfect conditions or "funny spots" like e.g. fenders that somehow disperse projectiles in the most unpredictable ways ("Get Gaijined / WarThundered" meme came to day for a reason...) - Tech trees are illogical, you e.g. research Ostwind first then Wilberwind, where Wilberwind is the actual precedessor to Ostwind, or like Pz. IV J (which is basically budget late-war Pz. IV H) is more expensive to repair than Panzer IV H, while retaining it's nerfed armour, turret rotation speed etc. - Vehicles are incredibly unbalanced, they can share similar BRs but one will be virtually useless (Marder IFVs, M56 Scorpion... Or well, see the Panzer IV J above) while other being basically W+M1 no-brainer (Raketenautomat, ZSU-57-2, etc.). Premiums are even worse than this, you can buy a gimmick that is hardly useful but also a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ like T58 or Obj. 279 that is virtually impossible to fight against (...Vell, unless you use another no-skill vehicle) - Most recent additions were copy-paste vehicles, I think the only thing that somewhat stands out is that one italian boat with a funny derp gun on low BRs... Otherwise it's mostly duplicates from the normal tech tree with fancy camouflages or, oh here we go... - ...GenAI-generated assets. Firstly it's NOT mentioned on the Steam page that WarThunder contains slop, violating ToS. Second thing... Ask seventy euros (!) for a tech-tree copycat tank with textures made by glorified hallucinating prediction algorithm that further accelerates pollution and displaces people from jobs because it creates inferior but "good enough" "art" substibute. Seventy euros. Why just not get a proper, *real* tank scale model by now? This is low for even Gaijin standards... Aside from that, As early as I can recall (2019) the pfps looked unnatural / unsettling, they presumably were generated as well with some of the earliest models. Unlike with previous case though, you at least don't have to pay absurd amount of money for them - Would almost forget, navals... Navals are too unappealing, it's basically a clicker game because aimbots do everything for you from destroying aircraft to other ships. Using warplanes is annoying because well, aimbots mow your wooden / metal bird down. The enemy doesn't even have to spot you for them to kill you... Generally, this game is simply not worth it, it's in wreck state and the dev greed is rampant. You didn't learn the first time. I continuely find myself returning to this game like an abusive relationship. It continuely proves to me that it does not deserve my time and effort and yet there exists nothing better. The company, Gaijin Entertainment is lazy, over confident and insulting to the entire community for the effort that we have to put in in order to get a digital combat vehicle. The effort to make new updates interesting has gone through the floor, the game has turned into a bastardized satarical version of itself, a parody if you prefer, with every negative issue that existed since its beta existing now, but to such a crueler degree. Chief among the issues is copy paste vehicles. In particular the copy paste premium vehicles that the snail insists you get to help speed up your grind, rather than addressing the fact that the grind is unfair with premium, and tortureous if you are a free to play. These vehicles are naturally completely the same, maybe a new camouflage to pretend its new, some wooden pallets maybe, outside of that the vehicles are identical, and the extra detailing could instead be added to the tech tree variant as a decoration, something that you can already do on certain vehicles, in particular premium ones. The ratio of vehicles per update over the course of the game has increasingly started shifting towards premium vehicles, because the snail knows that you will buy it to ease your grind. Rather than blaming the people, the 'Whales' as some of the community call those who purchase the vehicles, the problem can only be directed to the predatory tactics of Gaijin, who know very well that they can get more and more money out of the weaker willed people who for one reason or another may believe that they have to have them. The issue with the event vehicles and by extension the battle passes and monthly decals is that not everyone has the time to play to get all of the aformentioned, and of course the snail will more than happily take your money to ease the process for you, rather than addressing the fact that their promises of easing the grind, back when the game was review bombed in 2023, they've completely forgotten their promise to the community. No, it is a failure of Gaijin Entertainment to even include AI in any capacity in the game, regardless of if it simply a texture of a tank, or profile pictures. Of course one has to address the part where yes, Gaijin Entertainment is allowing us to play a game free of charge, and that they need to have income to develop, and maintain it. Except none of the money that goes into it from us, the community seems to be going into anything that looks like its developing or maintaining this game. the effort of quality control is through the floor, bugs and errors seemingly constant, issues like ghost shells STILL persist, after so many posts and claims of finally fixing them. I have played this game on and off for years now and most of that was using their launcher outside of Steam, hence the lower number of hours shown. This game has been steadily going downhill. The premiums used to just be slightly different versions of freely available vehicles or some special kind of vehicle that wouldn't really work in normal gameplay and is just for fun. Now, premiums are either copy-paste version of regular top tier vehicles (which they previously said they wouldn't do at all) or straight up nostalgia or fan baiting highly sought after vehicles in the game that they can then squeeze players for cash with. The last kind of premium is by far the worst kind: Blatantly broken or overpowered vehicles, usually in the USSR tech tree to fulfill some kind of Russo power fantasy and give players of that in-game nation primarily a broken OP vehicle to use that they will refuse to balance until it stops selling like hotcakes. The latest update has had undisclosed AI generated content that has been discovered that breaks Steam's policy on AI disclosure. There have been some textures found with watermarks from Gemini AI. The patch not only doesn't remove the textures, it simply hides the watermarks (which goes against Gemini AI's terms of service). It makes you wonder what else in the game is AI generated and not disclosed. As for the rest of the game, if you're looking for a realistic tank or air combat game, this ISN'T it. Don't believe the people that consistently claim that this game is realistic just because you don't see HP bars anywhere. The damage modeling is poor and has frequent issues and inconsistencies, built on a game engine that is over a decade old and is barely hanging on. Coupled with some of the most bizarre game direction decisions in the name of so-called "game balance" that further exposes how anachronistic and poorly realised the game has become. The problem isn't just at top tier either, for example, the early game with WW2 tanks and aircraft is broken now since they have also opted to give a WW2 Soviet fighter ammunition that was described on paper but never actually used IRL that basically one shots most WW2 tanks and we're expected to believe that is "balancing" the game somehow. Yeah there's no other PVP tank or air combat game like it out yet, but the second there is, this game should just be dropped like it's radioactive. But if you really want to play this, don't get too invested, you'll only end up disappointed.

Hollow Knight is genuinely good as a Metroidvania. The map design, progression, and exploration are strong, and if you mainly care about movement, upgrades, and that "keep pushing deeper" feeling, it delivers. Some things of what people praise about it didn’t work for me. The music never really clicked. It’s not bad, but it never made areas feel memorable to me, most of the time it leans into the same nostalgic vibe with piano and strings. My biggest issue is with the story and lore. It often feels cryptic just for the sake of being cryptic, like it wants to be mysterious even when being clearer would make it more engaging. The reason I didn’t rate it badly is simple. As a Metroidvania, the core gameplay is well made.

Just finished my first playthrough of this game and I figured I HAVE to leave a review considering how much I have enjoyed this adventure through the might Hallownest. This review will be completely SPOILER-FREE so I won't ruin anyone's first exprience with this. First of all, this is NOT an easy game, I found myself rather stressed at times where I couldn't traverse certain areas because of the insane amount of enemies there or when fighting different tough bosses. It is a game strongly focused on trial and error so if you don't like dying too much in video games, well you'll have quite a time here ;) Visually, the game is absolutely stunning, the artstyle is rather cute and friendly at times, but it can get gross and kinda disturbing at times too. The environment and the backgrounds of every place inside this game is absolutely breath-taking, not even starting to talk about the INSANE size of the map I figured lots of people tend to drop this game because it is not one of those games where you just have a pre-marked location with a waypoint and you just have to go there, the entire game is strictly based on exploration and it counts on YOU to unravel it's secrets. If you're a person that loves exploring, this is a great game for you. You will, however, meet lots of places that you can't pass through until you get a special ability to do so, there are a bunch of them and each one of them does something unique. So yeah, there IS a lot of backtracking and remembering stuff you discover. But hey, that is the metroidvania genre! ;) I had such a blast with this game and I look forward to buying the sequel as well. It can get really frustrating at times, but I 100% recommend pushing through it and experiencing this to it's very end.

Hollow Knight is a side-scrolling adventure game built around exploration and movement through connected areas. The visual style is hand-drawn and detailed, with a dark tone that gives each region a distinct identity. There is very little direct instruction on where to go, so progress mainly comes from paying attention to surroundings and slowly learning how different paths connect. At first it can feel unclear, but over time the layout starts to make more sense as new routes open after gaining movement abilities. Movement between areas often requires returning to earlier places once new skills are obtained, which changes how older sections are viewed. Each region has its own map structure that has to be uncovered step by step, and locating mapping points becomes important for keeping track of routes. The combat is based on timing and positioning rather than simple attacks, with enemies following different patterns that require adjustment in approach. It is a highly engaging experience that rewards patience and exploration, and is a strong choice for those who like interconnected world design and skill-based progression. I played the game using keyboard and mouse despite having an Xbox controller, because I personally prefer keyboard and mouse controls, even though I assume most players would choose a controller. To check and set up the keyboard and mouse binds, a screenshot of the layout is attached below as a reference for keyboard and mouse players. Overall, the game offers a large world with many hidden routes, optional paths, and multiple possible outcomes based on how much is discovered. The control system works well and allows precise movement during both platform sections and battles. It stands out as a well-crafted experience with attention to detail and steady exploration throughout the entire journey from start to finish. https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3733128589

Ahem... When I first started playing Hollow Knight, I was very bad at it. Very. Bad. However, this did not deter me, and little by little I continued onward throughout the game, slowly getting better at the platforming, at the combat, the fact that it was metrovania, a game genre I didn't know existed, I had just heard good things about it and wanted to try something I'd never tried before. While traveling through the world, I discovered a bug being eaten by an enormous, flying creature. I knew I had to save him, so after far too many tries, I saved him... I saved Zote. I expected gratitude, a thank you, or perhaps some cocky wanna be hero telling me he had it under control... I got the last one, but it was... ruder than I ever could have imagined... insulting, degrading, just a horrible, horrible bug... yet, I was fond of this horrible little creature. "Perhaps I'll encounter him again, and I'll be able to help him change into a better bug on my journey!" I thought to myself, and happily continued on my way, happy to have saved a life. Some time later, and after much more improvement, I found a hidden passage way in the Fungal wastes, and within it, at the top of what at the time was a very difficult platforming challenge for me, I saw a lost, scared, bug, clearly on the verge of being taken by the infection. I kept trying, and trying, and trying to traverse this not even difficult challenge, determined to save this bug, whoever they were, and return them to safety, and eventually, I did... I saved Bretta. Instantly I fell in love with Bretta (not romantically of course), she was so sweet, so shy, so lost, feeling completely and utterly forgotten, yet here I was, and I was now her only friend. Continuing on with my travels, I would frequently stop into Dirtmouth, and would always check on Bretta, for I didn't want her to feel alone, nor forgotten. Her infatuation with Ghost was honestly endearing to me, for she was never weird about it, she was never obsessive, nor odd, she was just shy, sweet, and quiet, even when her diary entries offered a completely different side of her. Even if her stories were exaggerated, even if they weren't real, I always looked forward to reading the next entry, for it was nice seeing someone so real within that small town. Dare I think it, I wanted to be the white knight she saw me as. Visiting Bretta became a wonderful tradition for me, and sitting beside her on the bench gave me a huge sense of connection to the world, a reason to always come back to Dirtmouth. Then I went into Deepnest. There, I once again discovered Zote in peril, and once again I rushed in to save him, hoping that perhaps this time he'd show a bit of character growth, or even a half baked attempt at a thank you. I did not receive this, I instead received insults, degradation, and rudeness. Still though, I just rolled my eyes, and thought to myself, "Oh Zote, when will you learn?" Thus, I continued on, happy to have saved a life again. Much later, once I was in the Colosseum of Fools, I once again found Zote, though this time locked in a cage, with once again nothing but insults to offer me. I laughed to myself, wished him luck, and entered the Challenge of the Warrior. I was mortified when at the end a huge, shaking cage dropped from above, only to burst into laughter when Zote appeared, waving his wooden sword pathetically and tripping on his own feet. Reluctantly, for I was worried I'd kill him, I did fight him, and sent him flying, sticking him in the ground by his own horns. "Oh Zote," I thought to myself, "when will you learn?" Thus, I continued on with my journeys, wondering when Zote would change... Then I got back to Dirtmouth. I expected to see Bretta sitting on the bench, or perhaps sleeping in her room, a new diary entry ready to be read, a new story about her white knight just waiting to be seen. I was so looking forward to seeing her again, it truly had been a long time, and I looked forward to our silent time together... What did I find? ZOTE. I found ZOTE, rambling on so loudly that the whole town could hear it, with Bretta... My dear, sweet, shy Bretta, blushing, listening to his monstrous, horrible, inflated lies, completely and utterly enamored. My world went black. Instantly I rushed into her room, only to find it had been overtaken by ZOTE. ZOTE not filled her journal, while any mentions of me were dashed to pieces by this horrible creatures lies, framing me as a cowardly villain within his own story. Images of ZOTE now filled her walls, the plushy of Ghost now replaced with something far more grotesque, more annoying, more... ZOTE. Just when all hope seemed lost, I then found it... a basement within Bretta's home... which held a great, paper mache stature of ZOTE with dream particles coming off of it. This was it, if I could beat this, I could rid myself of ZOTE. I quickly entered the battle, needle drawn and ready, prepared to face on whatever horrors awaited me... Or so I thought... I wasn't expecting to see Bretta atop a bed, being fanned and served by ZOTE'S... nor did I expect the GREY PRINCE ZOTE. I'll not go into detail, I simply cannot describe it, truly, it was... grotesque. However, in the end I vanquished the enemy, and upon emerging victorious, I quickly ran back to find Bretta, ready to hear her tell off Zote, to kick him out of the town, to find herself and her confidence! Instead, she now looked rather uninfatuated, bored even, with her once Grey Prince. Liking what I saw, but confused all the same, I returned to the basement, only to find that the dream particles were still there. Knowing what I had to do, I returned into that wretched abyss, ready to fight him however many times I needed to to win her back. I battled against ZOTE four times, each battle becoming more and more unfathomable, truly, grotesque in every way imaginable. When I earned my fourth victory, I emerged, hoping to find my Bretta, hoping to discover ZOTE gone, or being told off, something, anything! What I found instead shattered me to my very core. I found Bretta gone, and Zote still rambling on loudly to no one at all. Panicked, I searched her diary for answers, and to my horror discovered that she had indeed awakened from ZOTE'S spell... and left Hollownest entirely because of it in order to truly find herself. Gone. My dear, sweet, shy Bretta was gone... NOW REPLACED WITH ZOTE!!!!!!!! I did everything I could to make him leave, I listened to all his precepts, I fought his dream form until I simply couldn't do it anymore, I did EVERYTHING!!! But he just wouldn't leave. I was stuck with him, rambling for the whole town to hear, for the rest of my playthrough... While Bretta was gone forever. I was heartbroken. ... I've since started my second playthrough, and once again found myself staring up at ZOTE, being chewed on by that great, wonderful vengefly. I could have saved him, I'm better now, it wouldn't have been a problem, I'm a great player. Why on earth would I do that though, when I can keep Bretta safe in Dirtmouth, and perhaps more importantly, safe from ZOTE? When I can keep Dirtmouth safe from ZOTE? Ladies and gentlemen, non-binary friends, do you know why you should play Hollow Knight? The real reason you should play Hollow Knight? It's so that you can get the NEGLECT achievement, and truly, truly, keep Dirtmouth, and Hollownest for that matter as well as all it's inhabitants safe from that horrible, rude, insulting, lying, terrible, monstrous, ugly, belittling, manipulating, stupid, rambling, annoying, insidious bug known as ZOTE. Thank you for your time.

Hollow Knight is one of those rare games that completely pulled me in from start to finish. At first, I thought it was just another indie platformer, but the deeper I explored Hallownest, the more I realized how much care and passion went into every part of the game. The world feels alive despite being a fallen kingdom, with beautiful environments, memorable characters, and an atmosphere that constantly makes you want to see what's around the next corner. The exploration is incredibly rewarding because the game doesn't hold your hand. Every new area feels like a genuine discovery, and finding hidden paths or secrets always gave me a sense of accomplishment. The combat is simple but satisfying, and the boss fights are some of the best I've experienced in a Metroidvania game. Many of them took me multiple attempts, but defeating them never felt unfair—it felt earned. The soundtrack deserves special praise as well. It perfectly matches the mood of each area and made many moments even more memorable. While the game can be challenging and sometimes leaves you feeling lost, that's also part of what makes the adventure so special. If you enjoy exploration, challenging combat, and games that trust players to figure things out on their own, Hollow Knight is an easy recommendation. It's easily one of the best indie games I've ever played and absolutely worth every hour I spent in it.

I played Silksong before I played this game, so I will try to not compare the two, as both are extraordinarily unique and offer different experiences for many players, including myself. After beating Silksong, I moved on to this game. Looking back, I wish I had beaten this game before playing Silksong, since I didn't get much of the references in [spoiler]the Red Memory[/spoiler] but I did have some cool moments where I saw pieces of the story lining up. Other than that, this game is extremely well made. For a metroidvania, I kind of like the more linear gameplay (in my opinion...) and raw story that this game offers. Hollow Knight has well balanced and expertly themed areas, enemies, and songs that provide an epic, serene quality and ambiance that this game deserves. Every single one of the bosses are amazing (besides Grey Defenders), but I'm not sweaty enough to do the pantheons. I think the only thing slightly odd is that [spoiler]Dung Defender[/spoiler] has a different art style than everything else. It just feels the tiny bit out of place, but at the same time, it makes the game even more classic. There are some parts that feel a little repetitive and dull, and platforms that feel more like a maze than parkour, but that shouldn't stop you at all from enjoying yourself. This game is phenomenal. I recommend.

Why did it take me so long to play this game? I don't know. Do I regret not playing it sooner? Absolutely. I you like metroidvanias then you will like this one. It quite literally is a flagship game for the genre alongside metriod. Pros: Movement is fantastic. The music and atmosphere is on point. The art needs to be in a museum. The build options that can be done with the charms is incredible. The in game maps are done very well and the map markers in this edition are well done as well. The atmospheric storytelling that is done is also fantastic. I'm just waiting for a good enough sale to buy Silksong now! Cons: Essentially zero direction for what to do and where you need to go. I spent several hours on google and Reddit trying to understand what I needed to do to get the 118% completion. Also, some of the later game bosses were much more difficult than the final fight (If not doing true ending) And some of the pantheon fights and radiance fights are incredibly unfair and almost impossible to beat. (Granted they are 100% optional and supposed to be a challenge but for some of them, not getting hit is essentially impossible unlike path of pain which is just really difficult).

My girlfriend told me she liked the art style and visuals, so I downloaded it and we played through the game together all the way to 112%. It became my favorite indie game of all time and, in my opinion, the perfect metroidvania. The art design, characters, atmosphere, challenging combat, and platforming are all outstanding. You can tell this game was made with genuine passion and care. Unfortunately, Silksong didn't resonate with me (or my girlfriend) as much as the original, so it hasn't surpassed Hollow Knight for me—at least not yet. An absolute must-play. 10/10

The story is really good. I wish it told it to you. Many key plot details are presented through cryptic verse on hidden lore tablets or in dialogue that you can see exactly once per playthrough. This means you can piece things together through subsequent, attentive playthroughs reasonably well, but it means the story tends to sail over the head of the average player. This detracts from the emotional impact of certain lategame moments when experienced for the first time. As a longtime Hollow Knight fan, I wish the full potential here was realized, that's all. I feel that with a few changes to fix the lackluster storytelling, this could have been one of the great Story games around. But, given the superb soundtrack and environmental storytelling, the greatest map design of any metroidvania I know, and the best [spoiler] reason for a [/spoiler] silent protagonist in a game, this is still easily a 9.98/10.

У меня были цели, амбиции, друзья, нормальный режим сна и вера в человечество. Сейчас у меня осталась только ненависть к туману войны и привычка проверять миникарту даже когда я смотрю порно. ПЕРВОЕ ЗНАКОМСТВО Я помню свой первый матч. Меня встретили 9 человек, которые уже явно закончили минимум по три университета по токсичности, психологии давления и искусству унижения новичков. Я выбрал героя, нажал “рандом”, и игра сказала: «Поздравляем, ты теперь саппорт без понимания, что ты саппорт». Мой керри написал: “удали игру уёбок” Я ещё не знал, что это не совет. Это была философия. ОБУЧЕНИЕ Обучение в доту это когда тебе объясняют, как играть, а потом бросают в лес, как ребёнка в экономику. «Купи варды» Купил варды Поставил варды Умер, потому что поставил варды Керри: “репорт уёбку на саппе” Логика ясна. Варды нужны, чтобы ты видел момент своей смерти в пиздатом качестве. РАНГОВАЯ СИСТЕМА ММР - это не рейтинг. Это психологический эксперимент валв над тем, как долго человек может верить, что он поднимется, прежде чем начнёт пикать пуджа из принципа. Каждый матч - это: Один человек, который играет как TI победитель Один человек, который только что проснулся Один человек, который играет ногами И я, который пытается понять, почему мы все в одной команде ГЕЙМПЛЕЙ Dota 2 - это единственная игра, где: ты можешь выиграть 45 минут и проиграть за 7 секунд, потому что кто-то пошёл бить древних без байбека, трезвости и совести Каждая драка выглядит так: 1. Кто-то кричит «го выебем их ща» 2. Половина команды не готова 3. Вторая половина уже умерла 4. Я нажимаю телепорт на таверну, которого уже нет СОЮЗНИКИ Союзники в доте - это не команда. Это 4 незнакомца, объединённые только общей болью и отсутствием взаимопонимания. Типы союзников: Мидер: «Я смурф даблите» → 0/5 на 6-й минуте Керри: фармит лес, пока несут трон Саппорт: купил варды один раз и теперь философ Оффлейнер: ушёл в лес, потому что захотел ВРАГИ Враги всегда: знают тайминги читают карту любят тебя особенно когда ты на 10 HP и без телепорта ОБЩЕНИЕ Чат в доте — это место, где: советы выглядят как угрозы угрозы выглядят как религия и каждый второй — будущий участник судебного разбирательства, если бы валв добавила суд ПОБЕДЫ Иногда ты выигрываешь. Это происходит случайно, примерно как найти деньги в старой куртке. Ты не понимаешь почему победил, команда не понимает почему победила, и никто не чувствует, что это заслуженно. ПОРАЖЕНИЯ Поражения — это не конец. Это начало следующего матча, где ты уже морально сломан, но всё равно жмёшь поиск, потому что мозг уже подписал контракт с зависимостью. ИТОГ Dota 2 - это не игра. Это: психологический тренинг симулятор коллективной ответственности без ответственности школа терпения, злости и принятия неизбежного и единственное место, где фраза “gg ez” звучит как объявление войны Рекомендую. I've been playing Dota 2 for so long that at this point I'm not entirely sure whether I play Dota or Dota plays me. The weird thing is I don't even grind ranked anymore. Turbo is where it's at. My friends and I treat Dota 2 like that old reliable restaurant you've been going to for years. You know the menu. You know exactly what you're getting. You know you'll probably complain about it while you're there. And yet somehow you keep coming back. Every time a new game comes out, the group gets excited. "We're definitely playing this for months." Two weeks later the Discord call goes quiet. Somebody eventually says the magic words: "Dota?" And just like that, ten years of gaming innovation gets thrown into a ditch. Back to Turbo. Back to chaos. Back to five grown adults making terrible decisions at maximum speed. That's what makes Turbo so good. Nobody cares. People are picking heroes they haven't touched in three years. Someone is building items that should probably violate international law. Your friend swears his build is "secret tech" even though it's currently 4-12 and being investigated by multiple human rights organizations. And somehow it still works. Sometimes. The matches are fast enough that losing doesn't feel like you've wasted an evening. You get stomped? Whatever. Queue again. Twenty minutes later you're all laughing because a fully farmed support just deleted somebody from existence using what appears to be a kitchen utensil and raw confidence. The best part isn't even the game anymore. It's the people. Dota has become less of a game and more of a digital pub where the activity is yelling at each other while occasionally remembering to destroy the Ancient. Half the fun is listening to your friends confidently announce a terrible plan and then watching it unfold exactly as badly as expected. The game itself is somehow still amazing after all these years. Every match creates a different kind of stupidity. Some games feel like a tactical war. Others feel like five shopping carts crashing into each other in a supermarket parking lot. Both are equally entertaining. Sure, Dota has problems. The learning curve is less of a curve and more of a vertical cliff covered in broken glass. The community can occasionally make you question the future of humanity. And every patch somehow manages to make heroes stronger, weaker, stronger again, then somehow turn a support into a raid boss. But none of that really matters. Because when the group has nothing new to play, Dota is always there. Waiting. Patiently. Like an old friend. Or a highly intelligent curse. Right. Time to queue Turbo and immediately regret trusting my teammates with anything more complicated than breathing.

I can’t recommend this game anymore. The community is one of the most toxic I’ve ever experienced. The matchmaking and ranking system feels completely broken, no matter how much you improve, it often feels like you're stuck in the same place forever. Climbing MMR rarely feels rewarding, and too many games are decided by factors outside of your control. Cheaters seem more common than ever, and the game is full of griefers, trolls, and players who clearly have no interest in winning. The worst part is that many matches feel decided within the first 10 minutes because someone gives up, starts feeding, or intentionally ruins the game. Yet you're still forced to sit through another 40+ minutes of a miserable and completely unwinnable match. Dota 2 is still a great game at its core, but the overall experience has become exhausting. Too many matches are ruined by toxic behavior instead of being decided by skill. After thousands of hours, I no longer have fun playing it. I'd rather spend $100 on another game than queue up for another match of Dota 2.

Was a great game once, but it's become overrun with bots. I approached Dota 2 with genuine interest, hoping to understand its celebrated complexity through Turbo matches—a supposedly lower-stakes environment. Instead, I was met with relentless toxicity. Despite making honest efforts to learn, I was spam-reported simply for not playing perfectly. The result? A communications ban, an 8-hour matchmaking suspension, and even an Overwatch penalty—all for the crime of being inexperienced. The community doesn’t teach; it harasses. Every mistake is met with ping-spamming and verbal abuse. The automated systems don’t distinguish between intentional griefing and beginner struggles—they just weaponize mob rule against newcomers. If Valve intends Dota 2 to have any future, this is a catastrophic failure. League of Legends, for all its flaws, offers a more constructive path to learning. It outclasses Dota in every meaningful way—approachability, community management, and basic respect for a player’s time. As for Dota’s community: it deserves the same neglect Valve has shown Team Fortress 2—abandonment by developers, overrun by bots, and left to rot. Even that would be too kind.

after so much time of playing a game I liked I am starting to think icefrog stopped working on the project or he really stopped looking at anything else but the international. Maybe just the statistics on a dashboard. Recent changes are just retarded, the facets were awesome. YES AWESOME. It gave heroes that had nothing to do with other roles like core mid for example the option to be viable and with enough skill awesome. Only reason to lose ending up making your team play anything else than go jungle farm farm farm like a idiot and come back with items. You had timings matter for a second in different ways. Case and point Warlock mid. If enough skill and farming you could time your way to a fast win if left alone. Team and smarts were required. In forced pugs to play more team if they want to win. And I felt people try and be more open with time to new ideas. Now nerfs after nerfs. Go back and play "support" roles. The best part about dota is that you can if you have the skill do whatever the hell you want and make it happen. THIS MUST ALWAYS BE DOTA. This the fcking game I love. But apparently dota is trailing league or whatever. Go for that finer pasture and lose all of us hard core fans in the process see where that take you. Also your stories need GRIT, not California storytelling or other bs. We are not interested in your strong independent woman, if you know you know. We are here for strong characters that prove they paid there fair share of effort to be where they are. Also Some skills become so fundamentally tied to the hero that they should never ever be removed. Like the execute skill in early game wind ranger. Fantastic. Literally the piece that allowed for a more mage style wr play with a damage transition into the late game. If you got timing right you could go with a maelstrom and than go for a linken blink combo run wild on the map buy your core time to farm the farm. And finish the game. If you failed the timing no real dmg until you got some heavy hitting stuff. Strong early(that's if you hit your arrows, which is not as easy if people stay on their feet) meh mid and strong late. Great stuff, well balanced. The changes adds the speed that is like a 10% increase losing the core thing that you were always missing in the early game. Hitting the arrow is never easy in a more competitive setting and now when you do it in spite of the difficulty you do nothing. No rewards for guessing correctly no rewards of doing something outside of the box. You get it at level 25. Yea when the game is done, thanks mate. I never felt like something so fundamental missing. It's like removing primal split from brewmaster. The crit from Phantom assassin. Instead of removing the facets you should have doubled down on them. Give people options to play around with. Fun means fun. But whatever, I suppose people were right when they said the game is dying. As someone aptly put it. There are not 4 idiots in your team sometimes but 5 with myself included for playing. Gg wp no re for all the old timers out there.

If you get reported by a number of people, you get placed in low priority queue. This can happen in any game, even non-ranked for any reason. Someone doesn't like the way you play, items you picked, or your power went out and you left two separate games but even though you reconnected the damage was done. Now in Low priority, you can only play single draft, which means you are not allowed to play any heroes you want, only select from a randomized 4 heroes. matchmaking times are longer, and to make matters worse, to get out of this punishment, you have to WIN BETWEEN 1-5 GAMES. Imagine you are new, or coming back after years. Every game you get stomped, about 30minutes in and you know there is no coming back, they farm kills on everyone just for fun. Now imagine you are in a queue full of bad players, you yourself are bad at the game, and you cannot get out of the queue unless you get lucky. So your potentially stuck in this queue forEVER if you never win. Do you see how stupid this is? they also took it a step further by taking away achievements, exp and anything gained for completing a match. Every game in dota 2 is roughly an hour + long endeavour. This means you are committing to losing several matches, AND you don't get to play the heroes you want to play. How can a game be enjoyable with that sort of punishment? This punishment is reserved for players with a string of reports, so like I said before, you are bad, you are losing, you are learning, but some sweaty, who belongs in RANKED, is playing all pick normal casual game and reporting you for not getting an item on a character. they are flaming you in chat and threatening to report you. So their toxic behavior benefits them, especially if they queue with friends that can mass report you. I guess the lesson here is if you want to play dota 2, do not play any game besides the new player queue, and if you decided to go beyond that queue, make sure you queue with friends so you cannot be mass reported. If you do not have dota 2 friends, uninstall the game, and voice your complaints to gabe newell.

Its a game with a dangerous combination, its quite addicting but fundamentally broken at a root level, perhaps by design. The game itself at its core is fun, the heroes are fun to play, and the game is updated semi-often (although the quality and quantity of said updates has significantly declined in the last few years). Theirs no game quite like Dota 2, theirs over 100 heroes, all of them play completely differently, with their own skills and item builds, no two heroes feel the game. When playing with a stack theirs quite alot of thought that goes into your draft, your item build and your skill build. When it clicks its very addictive and quite honestly very fun. Nothing gives you a better dopamine hit than getting a rampage. Or winning that really close 50 minute game. But good lord the games horribly designed, to begin with, this game is unbelievably complicated, staring at the screen, youll see numbers and graphics everywhere, even basic skills and abilities can have half a page of text explaining what they do, items can too. This isnt a casual game that you play for a little while and move on, this is a game you play for life, or you dont play it at all. Even before you pick a hero to play, the fundamental basics of the game take months to learn, your looking at years of investment before your anywhere close to "good", thats just how complicated and in-depth this game is. If you want to play a new hero for example, youll need to read guides, watch videos and replays. You might be ok on a hero initially, but without going off and doing a bunch of research, you'll just end up throwing games and having people rage at you, this isnt a game, its a second job, in most cases it feels like your primary job. Then theirs the large patches that come out every 2-3 months, Dota 2 patches can be quite big. Dozens of pages and notes affecting huge amounts of heroes, items and even the map. Every time a release comes out you may have your favourite item or hero completely reworked entirely, meaning you effectively have to learn them again every 6-12 months, depending on the hero. Even worse, some patches completely change the basics of the game so drastically it feels like your playing a whole new game, this happens often. But perhaps the biggest design flaw of this game is the way it handles advantages in the early and mid game. When you get kills, you get money and experience, same with towers. Money lets you buy items to get stronger, experience helps you level up so you can get more stats and abilities. If one team gets an early lead, assuming a somewhat equal draft, then coming back is almost impossible since just being ahead gives you an advantage so you can get even more ahead, Dota 2 is an EXTREMELY snowball heavy game, once the snowball starts rolling, stopping it becomes almost impossible. The old saying "whoever gets ahead stays ahead" should be the motto of this game. In the rare case you do manage to pull off a comeback, its because the enemy team has made a series of major mistakes to let your team back in, rarely is it because you “outplayed” anyone. This snowball methodology becomes so laughable that even if you do outplay the other team, you often wont get a kill just because the enemy team is so tanky, and your own items are so far behind that kills becomes significantly more difficult. The game actively prohibits better teams winning if they lose the mid/eary game because they are just too far behind to catchup. Cores with a gold/experience lead can legitimately wipe 3-4 enemy heroes on their own. Think of it this way, your playing tennis and you get a break of serve, your now ahead, as a result of you getting that break of serve, your own serve is now 25% faster, you move 25% quicker and your 25% less tired. This is the basic premise in Dota 2, you get a significant advantage just for being ahead, this happens in no other sport, you dont get to move 25% faster just because you sunk a three pointer in basketball. You dont get to throw a pitch 25% faster because you just struck someone out previously in a game of baseball. Moreover, stomps are VERY common in this game. 4/5 games are stomps where you know whose going to win after the first 10 or so minutes, where Dota 2 shines is that 1/5 game that goes down to the wire and you win. The addiction comes when you try to chase that same feeling and game for the next 3 days in vain. The other critical flaw in this game is just how minimal you can feel in terms of impact. You are 1 of 10 players, so even though you may be playing a great game, it doesnt matter if two other people on your team are just playing for laughs and giggles and end up dying alot in the early part of the game. Conversely, youll often win games where you play terribly, because someone else in your team is stomping everyone else. The only role that is absolutely critical is the position 1 role, you can win games with bad supports or an average mid, but you wont win with a terrible position 1. This often means that support players often feel like wins are out of their hands, it can be incredibly deflating when your playing fantastic as a support, but your position 1 feed 5 kills in the opening 2 minutes. The other aspect of this game that often gets overlooked is just how toxic games can be. This isnt COD or BF2 where games last 15 minutes, you can leave whenever you want and no one really talks because its just a goofy, fun game where no one really cares that much. Dota 2 games can last 50 minutes, thats a long time to be stuck with the same 4 people on your team, if things are going badly, people can get toxic very quickly, and you cant leave or quit before the game ends since theres no surrender button. If your behind as per my above point, comebacks just dont happen that often, and as a result its not uncommon to be sitting in a game that was over 15 minutes ago, just watching you and your team mates die over and over again for 20 minutes. This game brings out the worst in people, you absolutely have to have very thick skin to play this game, its not for the timid or shy. In the end, I have over 9000 hours in this game, most of the time I have not enjoyed, but due to how addicting it can be, stopping becomes challenging. If you do take the plunge and dedicate serious amounts of time and effort into this game, quitting becomes even harder because youve already dedicated a huge amount of time already, time you dont want to feel like youve "wasted". I often think about what I could have done with those 9000 hours, what skills I could have developed or interests I could have pursued. Dont be like me, I hate this game and I wish I never played it.
